channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Elevated errors across many models
item.description: Jun 22, 19:20 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 22, 19:14 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Three things to watch amid Anthropic’s latest feud with the government
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. For those of you enjoying your summer unaware of Anthropic’s latest feud with the US government, here’s a recap: In April the company said it had built an AI...

item.title: Increased Error Rates
item.description: Jun 22, 16:50 UTC Monitoring - Traffic engineering efforts have successfully mitigated the majority of congestion and packet drops. Jun 22, 16:30 UTC Update - Traffic engineering efforts have successfully mitigated the majority of congestion and packet drops. Services are largely stable, with only...

item.title: Cloudflare is investigating an issue where customers are being blocked deploying Managed Rules
item.description: Jun 22, 15:15 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 22, 15:01 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 22, 14:25 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 22, 14:24 UTC Investigating - We are currently...

item.title: Replicate elevated error rate
item.description: Jun 22, 15:00 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 22, 14:49 UTC Monitoring - Cloudflare has identified an issue where customers using Replicate.com may experience an elevated error rate

item.title: GLM-5.2 is the step change for open agents
item.description: A capability threshold I've been carefully monitoring.

item.title: Decades-Old Squid Proxy Flaw ‘Squidbleed’ Can Expose User Data
item.description: Squidbleed, discovered with the aid of Claude Mythos Preview, has been described as a Heartbleed-style vulnerability. The post Decades-Old Squid Proxy Flaw ‘Squidbleed’ Can Expose User Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: New OXLOADER Loader Uses Malicious Google Ads to Deliver CastleStealer
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new campaign that delivers CastleStealer by means of a previously unreported malware loader dubbed OXLOADER. According to Elastic Security Labs, the campaign leverages malicious Google Ads as a starting point to distribute the malware. Evidence...

item.title: PP-OCRv6 on Hugging Face: 50-Language OCR from 1.5M to 34.5M Parameters
Google Sets Sept. 30 Deadline for Android Developer Verification in Four Countries
item.description: Google has set September 30, 2026, as the day it begins enforcing Android developer verification in the first four countries, and the major device-maker app stores are in from the start. On that date, certified Android phones in Brazil, Indonesia, Singapore, and Thailand will block normal...

item.title: The Download: record-breaking subsea tunnels and flexible data centers
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Inside the world’s deepest and longest subsea road tunnel —Niall Firth I’m currently around 1,000 feet beneath the North Sea, in a dark, dank cave....

item.title: The AI shift in cyber risk: why leaders must act now
Stop Your Legacy Infrastructure from Hijacking Your AI Agents
item.description: Earlier this month, I spoke at the Gartner Security & Risk Management Summit about a blind spot most security programs are still not accounting for - how attackers are circumventing AI security programs by using legacy infrastructure to hijack AI agents. AI adoption is moving faster than...

item.title: Attackers Exploit Gravity SMTP Plugin Flaw to Harvest Valuable WordPress Data
item.description: Vulnerable WordPress plugin iterations leak API keys, secrets, tokens, server information, and other data. The post Attackers Exploit Gravity SMTP Plugin Flaw to Harvest Valuable WordPress Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: North Korean Hackers Blamed for Mastra NPM Supply Chain Attack
item.description: A malicious dependency the attackers added to over 140 Mastra packages fetches a payload targeting cryptocurrency extensions. The post North Korean Hackers Blamed for Mastra NPM Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Browser Bugs, EDR Killers, TV Botnet, OpenBSD Flaw, Android Trojan, and More
item.description: It’s Monday again. This week’s threat list looks painfully familiar: abused integrations, fake tools, poisoned websites, ransomware crews trying to shut down security tools, and mobile malware asking for way too much control. The annoying part is how little of this feels new. Weak credentials,...

item.title: What the Latest ShinyHunters Breaches Reveal About Modern Cyberattacks
item.description: Groups like ShinyHunters are demonstrating that attackers do not necessarily need malware or zero-day exploits to cause massive damage. The post What the Latest ShinyHunters Breaches Reveal About Modern Cyberattacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: New Exploit Bypasses Apple’s Boot Defenses, Affects Millions of iPhones
item.description: The vulnerability exploited by the Usbliter8 exploit cannot be patched and a PoC exploit has been released by researchers. The post New Exploit Bypasses Apple’s Boot Defenses, Affects Millions of iPhones appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated errors for Claude Opus 4.8
item.description: Jun 22, 09:59 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 22, 08:12 UTC Update - We are continuing to investigate this issue. Jun 22, 08:11 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Canada’s Spy Agency Used First-of-Its-Kind Warrant to Clean Botnet-Infected Devices
item.description: Canada's spy service got a judge's permission to reach into infected servers, home routers, and IoT gear sitting on Canadian soil and neutralize two foreign-run botnets. The Federal Court released a public version of the ruling on June 15. It is the first time the Canadian Security...

item.title: CaoCao Mobility Joins Doubao Ride-Hailing Gray Test as AI Assistant Expands Into Real-World Services
item.description: ByteDance's Doubao AI assistant begins gray-testing ride-hailing services through CaoCao Mobility, marking another step in the platform's evolution from content AI to a gateway for physical-world services.

item.title: Zhipu AI Breaches Trillion Yuan Market Cap: What Is Driving the Surge?
item.description: Zhipu AI's Hong Kong-listed shares surged past HKD 2,380, pushing its market capitalization beyond HKD 1 trillion. A deep dive into the fundamentals, market dynamics, and risks behind the stunning valuation.

item.title: Top 30 Domestic AI Computing Chips Unveiled: Zhongcheng Hualong and XiWang Accelerate IPO Processes
item.description: The 2026 Top 30 Domestic AI Computing Chips list reveals a maturing industry with clear tier segmentation, as Zhongcheng Hualong and XiWang push toward public listings amid surging demand for domestic computing power.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Blocked for Non-US Users: Why Domestic AI Is the Only Safe Bet for China
item.description: The US government's sudden order blocking foreign access to Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 signals a new era of AI export control, reinforcing the strategic importance of China's domestically developed AI models.

item.title: Xiaomi vs Huawei On-Device AI: Decoding the AI Strategies of 8 Major Smartphone Giants
item.description: The on-device AI battle among major smartphone makers intensifies as Xiaomi and Huawei lead with distinct approaches to mobile AI, from MiMo-V2.5 to Pangu models.

item.title: Inside the world’s deepest and longest subsea road tunnel
item.description: It’s cold, it’s very, very noisy, and—if I can be quite honest with you—I’m not feeling super relaxed. I’m currently around 300 meters, or 1,000 feet, beneath the North Sea, in a dark, dank cave. It smells weird. And I am increasingly aware of the pressure from millions of tons of seawater just...

item.title: Elevated Error Rates for Opus 4.8, Opus 4.7, Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6, and Haiku 4.5
item.description: Jun 22, 02:06 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 22, 02:01 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 22, 01:56 UTC Update - Opus 4.7 has recovered Jun 22, 01:33 UTC Update - Haiku 4.5 has recovered Jun 22, 01:16 UTC Update - Opus 4.8 has...

item.title: [Exclusive] $250 off AI Engineer tix til Monday
item.description: special offer for subscribers - $250 off AI Engineer tix til Monday

item.title: SSL Certificate Provisioning Delays
item.description: Jun 21, 14:01 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 21, 13:46 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is investigating multiple reports of advanced certificate issuance delays

item.title: Opus 4.8 errors
item.description: Jun 20, 17:23 UTC Update - We are continuing to investigate this issue. Jun 20, 17:07 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: [AINews] not much happened today
item.description: a quiet day lets us promo AIE one last time

item.title: Analyzing the Narration Gap in LLM-Solver Loops
item.description: arXiv:2606.19588v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Formal tools such as SAT and SMT solvers are increasingly embedded in language model reasoning pipelines when a safety or security critical question can be formulated in logic. Unlike chain of thought whose steps are sampled from the model...

item.title: Configurable Clinical Information Extraction with Agentic RAG: What Works, What Breaks, and Why
item.description: arXiv:2606.19602v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Patient contexts span hundreds of heterogeneous documents and thousands of structured data points, yet the document-level metadata that AI systems need for retrieval and triage is absent or incomplete. Standard retrieval-augmented generation fails on...

item.title: Which Pairs to Compare for LLM Post-Training?
item.description: arXiv:2606.19607v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Preference-based post-training has become a central paradigm for aligning language models. A common data-collection strategy is to generate a small set of completions for each prompt and label the resulting comparison pairs. However, human preference...

item.title: Toten: Knowledge-Based Ontological Tokenization Of Physical Quantities And Technical Notation In Brazilian Portuguese
item.description: arXiv:2606.19626v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Byte-Pair Encoding tokenization is statistically efficient for vocabulary compression, but semantically blind to structured technical entities, fragmenting physical quantities, numbers, units, and symbolic expressions into lexically arbitrary...

item.title: AI4SE and SE4AI Exploration: A Decade Looking Back and Forward
item.description: arXiv:2606.19630v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The March 2020 INCOSE INSIGHT special issue on AI and Systems Engineering (SE) became the most downloaded issue in the publication's history and launched a research community that now draws over 250 registrants to its annual workshop. In this article,...

item.title: Granularity-Regulated Adaptive Computational Efficiency for Optimal Verification in Test-Time Scaling
item.description: arXiv:2606.19354v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Test-time scaling (TTS) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for improving the reasoning performance of large language models (LLMs) by investing additional compute at inference time. A central component of TTS is the \emph{verifier}, which selects or...

item.title: Trustworthy Multi-Agent Systems: Mitigating Semantic Drift with the Argent Signaling Protocol
item.description: arXiv:2606.19356v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: When multi-agent LLM systems produce bad answers, not all failures are equal: some answers are grounded in the right material but incomplete, while others are simply ungrounded and should be stopped. Current retry strategies treat both cases...

item.title: Characterizing Narrative Content in Web-scale LLM Pretraining Data
item.description: arXiv:2606.19468v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The narrative composition of web-scale LLM pretraining corpora remains largely unexplored even though narrative is a fundamental mode of human communication. We present the first fine-grained study of narrative features in Dolma, a 3-trillion-token...

item.title: Reliability without Validity: A Systematic, Large-Scale Evaluation of LLM-as-a-Judge Models Across Agreement, Consistency, and Bias
item.description: arXiv:2606.19544v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-as-a-Judge has become the dominant evaluation paradigm for language models, but judge validation in practice relies on exact-match agreement, a metric that does not correct for chance and systematically overstates discriminative ability. We...

item.title: LaViSA: A Language and Vision Structural Ambiguity Benchmark
item.description: arXiv:2606.19552v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Structural ambiguity arises when a single sentence admits multiple valid interpretations due to its syntactic structure, posing a fundamental challenge for language understanding. Visual scenes serve as useful cues for resolving such ambiguity, and...

item.title: New subscriptions may not be correctly activated
item.description: Jun 19, 23:18 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 19, 23:14 UTC Update - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 19, 23:13 UTC Update - We are continuing to monitor for any further issues. Jun 19, 23:13 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are...

item.title: Banning Open Source AI Would Be A Mistake
item.description: This post was originally an op-ed co-authored with Kevin Xu of Interconnected for a general, non-technical audience.

item.title: The Download: AI bottleneck debates, and BCI trials take off
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. A startup claims it broke through a bottleneck that’s holding back LLMs AI startup Subquadratic came out of stealth last month with a huge claim: it...

item.title: A startup claims it broke through a bottleneck that’s holding back LLMs
item.description: Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic came out of stealth mode last month with a huge claim. It announced that it had solved a mathematical bottleneck that had been holding back large language models for almost a decade. The details were thin, and many people were unconvinced. But Subquadratic has...

item.title: Elevated error rates on the Claude API
item.description: Jun 19, 08:45 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 19, 08:37 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 19, 08:17 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: [AINews] GLM > GPT? GLM-5.2 passes vibe check; Z.ai forecasts Open Fable by December
item.description: With GLM-5.2 passing everyone's vibe check, the open models story finally becomes a real frontier story.

item.title: Computational Identifiability
item.description: arXiv:2606.19361v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Identification conditions describe the computability of a target query or parameter of interest as a function of the type and amount of information available. In causal identification, this information is often expressed in the form of a causal graph,...

item.title: When to Trust, How to Distill: Multi-Foundation Model Guidance for Lightweight, Robust Scientific Time Series Forecasting
item.description: arXiv:2606.19363v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The deployment of Time-Series Foundation Models (TSFMs) in physical sciences is hindered by a critical trade-off: while these models encode rich, universal temporal dynamics, they suffer from severe distributional misalignment when applied zero-shot...

item.title: Closing the Social-Semantic Gap: SPSD for Edge-Based Prompt Compression in Cloud LLM Inference
item.description: arXiv:2606.19364v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The prefill stage of Large Language Model (LLM) inference is a growing contributor to cloud-scale energy cost. Many consumer-support and conversational prompts contain social scaffolding: politeness markers, apologetic preamble, repetition, and...
